LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT SHE KNOWS MS. ELIZABETH SALVESON AND IS A SOCIAL FRIEND. THEY PRACTICED LAW AT THE SAME LAW FIRM AT HOWARD RICE FROM 1981 THROUGH 1987 AND FROM 1992 THROUGH 1995. THIS FACT DOES NOT AFFECT THE COURT'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. THE COURT GAVE COUNSEL THE OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTIONS. NO QUESTIONS WERE ASKED OR OBJECTIONS WERE RAISED. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PLAINTIFF ROBERTS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS CANNOT ESTABLISH THE RACIAL HARRASSMENT OR FAILURE TO PREVENT HARRASSMENT CAUSE OF ACTION. THE RACIAL SLUR ON THE SIGN IN SHEET WAS AN ISOLATED INCIDENT. THE COURT GRANTS AS TO THE RETALIATION AND DISCRIMINATION CAUSES OF ACTION BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PROVE AN ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G) AND APPROVAL AS TO FORM.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN W. HARRINGTON OF THE LAW OFFICES OF WAUKEEN MCCOY, TEL. NO. 675-7705 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. TERENCE J. HOWZELL, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3960 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING - Judge Miller disclosed that she knows Ms. Elizabeth Salveson and is a social friend. They practiced law at the same law firm at Howard Rice from 1981 through 1987 and from 1992 through 1995. This fact does not affect the Court's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. The Court gave counsel the opportunity to ask questions. No questions were asked or objections were raised. The matter was argued. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PLAINTIFF REDD is granted. Plaintiffs cannot establish the racial harassment or failure to prevent harassment cause of action. The racial slur on the sign-in sheet was an isolated incident. The Court grants as to the retaliation cause of action because plaintiff fails to prove an adverse employment action. (Moving party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G) AND APPROVAL AS TO FORM) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/mjm

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN W. HARRINGTON OF THE LAW OFFICES OF WAUKEEN MCCOY, TEL. NO. 675-7705 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. TERENCE J. HOWZELL, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3960 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING - Judge Miller disclosed that she knows Ms. Elizabeth Salveson and is a social friend. They practiced law at the same law firm at Howard Rice from 1981 through 1987 and from 1992 through 1995. This fact does not affect the Court's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. The Court gave counsel the opportunity to ask questions. No questions were asked or objections were raised. The matter was argued. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PLAINTIFF ROBERTS is granted. Plaintiffs cannot establish the racial harrassment or failure to prevent harrassment cause of action. The racial slur on the sign in sheet was an isolated incident. The court grants as to the retaliation and discrimination causes of action because plaintiff fails to prove an adverse employment action. (Moving party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G) and approval as to form.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN W. HARRINGTON OF THE LAW OFFICES OF WAUKEEN MCCOY, TEL. NO. 675-7705 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATY. TERENCE J. HOWZELL, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3960 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTENATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to plaintiff Redd for the race discrimination claim. Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to plaintiff Roberts for the disability discrimination and failure to accommodate claims. Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied as to plaintiff Roberts for the race discrimination claim. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFFS TROY ROBERTS AND LINDA REDD.
Appearances: Terence J. Howzell of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3960) for defendant and moving party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: defendant's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Govt. Code 815.2 is not an authorizing statute and plaintiff cannot plead the violation of a mandatory duty. One order on demurrer and motion to strike signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES.
Appearances: Terence J. Howzell of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3960) for defendant and moving party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: defendant's motion to strike is granted. Plaintiff concedes that punitive damages against a public entity are not permitted. Therefore, the prayer for punitive damages as to this defendant is stricken. One order on demurrer and motion to strike signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
